title: TCP port accepted as u16 but only validated at connect() not at construction
description: Transport::Tcp { port: u16 } accepts any u16 value at struct construction. Ports above 32767 are only rejected inside Client::connect() with a BadArgs error referencing 'C transport uses i16'. A user who builds a Transport::Tcp from a config file, stores it, and passes it to connect() much later gets a confusing error that references an internal C type. The port 32767 limit is lower than the IANA/OS valid range (1-65535) and will surprise anyone using a port like 50000 from a standard config. This will generate recurring user confusion issues.
